techniques.evidence_quote: gained entry into the AWS-based Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network via a SonicWall SMA 500v EC2 instance allowing inbound traffic on any port
SonicWall SMA 500v EC2 instance allowing inbound traffic on any port
the EC2 instance started scanning the environment
attempted to pivot to other internal systems via RDP
downloading hundreds of GBs of data over SMB from another EC2 instance
downloading hundreds of GBs of data over SMB from another EC2 instance
compromised instances uploaded data over internal RDP connections to other instances, which then started transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint
transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint over port 5000, which is typically used for Universal Plug and Play (UPnP)
received HTTPS connections from three rare Vultr VPS endpoints
transfer more than 230 GB of data to the rare external GTHost VPS endpoint
they detonated ransomware within the compromised VPC networks

attack_chain.description: Threat actor gained entry into the AWS VPC via a SonicWall SMA 500v EC2 instance that was allowing inbound traffic on any port. The instance received HTTPS connections from three rare Vultr VPS endpoints (45.32.205.52, 207.246.74.166, 45.32.90.176).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: gained entry into the AWS-based Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network via a SonicWall SMA 500v EC2 instance allowing inbound traffic on any port

attack_chain.description: Compromised SonicWall EC2 instance began scanning the internal environment to identify other systems and services.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the EC2 instance started scanning the environment

evidence_chunks.quote: the EC2 instance started scanning the environment
the EC2 instance started scanning the environment

attack_chain.description: Attacker pivoted from the compromised SonicWall EC2 instance to internal EC2 systems including a DC EC2 instance via RDP.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attempted to pivot to other internal systems via RDP, notably a DC EC2 instance

evidence_chunks.quote: attempted to pivot to other internal systems via RDP, notably a DC EC2 instance
attempted to pivot to other internal systems via RDP, notably a DC EC2 instance

attack_chain.description: The DC EC2 instance also began scanning the network after being compromised via RDP.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a DC EC2 instance, which also started scanning the network

evidence_chunks.quote: a DC EC2 instance, which also started scanning the network
a DC EC2 instance, which also started scanning the network

attack_chain.description: Attacker collected hundreds of GBs of data via SMB from another EC2 instance.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: downloading hundreds of GBs of data over SMB from another EC2 instance

evidence_chunks.quote: downloading hundreds of GBs of data over SMB from another EC2 instance
downloading hundreds of GBs of data over SMB from another EC2 instance

attack_chain.description: Compromised instances uploaded data over internal RDP connections to other EC2 instances, which were then used for exfiltration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: compromised instances uploaded data over internal RDP connections to other instances, which then started transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint

evidence_chunks.quote: compromised instances uploaded data over internal RDP connections to other instances, which then started transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint
compromised instances uploaded data over internal RDP connections to other instances, which then started transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint

attack_chain.description: Data was transferred to the external GTHost VPS endpoint over port 5000, typically used for UPnP.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint over port 5000, which is typically used for Universal Plug and Play (UPnP)

evidence_chunks.quote: transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint over port 5000, which is typically used for Universal Plug and Play (UPnP)
transferring data to the same GTHost VPS endpoint over port 5000, which is typically used for Universal Plug and Play (UPnP)

attack_chain.description: A total of 718 GB of data was exfiltrated to the external GTHost VPS endpoint 23.150.248.189 (initial transfer of 230+ GB).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor uploading a total of 718 GB to the external endpoint

evidence_chunks.quote: the threat actor uploading a total of 718 GB to the external endpoint
the threat actor uploading a total of 718 GB to the external endpoint

attack_chain.description: After exfiltration, the threat actor detonated ransomware within the compromised VPC networks. Darktrace's Autonomous Response was not configured for these devices so the activity was not blocked.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they detonated ransomware within the compromised VPC networks

evidence_chunks.quote: they detonated ransomware within the compromised VPC networks
they detonated ransomware within the compromised VPC networks

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of an internet-exposed SonicWall SMA 500v EC2 instance configured to allow inbound traffic on any port; HTTPS connections originated from rare Vultr VPS endpoints.
persistence_mechanism: Maintained foothold via the compromised SonicWall appliance and lateral RDP access to additional EC2 instances including a domain controller.
privilege_escalation: Not explicitly described; attacker leveraged lateral movement to a domain controller EC2 instance.
